headline: The Reapers Are Coming For Everyone (And the Wonderdogs Are Already Dead)
subheadline: Week 2 gave us a 9–0 demolition, a 40-run offensive explosion, and the most confusing 5–4 win in recent memory — welcome to the chaos season.
body:
## Thriller of the Week

### BaseballPro77 def. Benchwarmers, 5–4

Look, nobody watches a heist movie for the guy who almost gets away with it — but the **Benchwarmers** nearly pulled off the most improbable almost-robbery of Week 2. **BaseballPro77** somehow escaped with a 5–4 win despite posting a catastrophic 5.28 ERA, a 1.43 WHIP, and a grand total of *two home runs* (two! in a week! in 2025!). The **Benchwarmers** outclassed them in OBP (.343 vs .277), runs scored (25–19), and general aesthetic baseball vibes, yet still couldn't close it out thanks to a pitching staff that went 46 innings with a 4.89 ERA — so basically two bad bullpens walked into a bar and one of them got luckier. For **BaseballPro77**, this is back-to-back wins with the underlying stats of a team that's one bad week away from a full identity crisis. For the **Benchwarmers**, a date with the **Riverside Roughnecks** next week feels like the universe really not wanting them to figure things out.

---

## The Week's Defining Moment

The **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** didn't just win this week. They *erased* someone. A 9–0 sweep of the **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** is the fantasy baseball equivalent of the "Curb Your Enthusiasm" theme playing while someone's entire roster collapses in slow motion — and the numbers back it up completely. The Reapers posted a 2.48 ERA, a jaw-dropping 0.84 WHIP (that's not a typo, that's a *flex*), 75 strikeouts, and 8 saves/holds. This is a pitching staff that is currently performing like it has a personal vendetta against every hitter in the league.

The **Wonderdogs**, meanwhile, posted zero stolen bases — *zero*, not like a bad week, literally a goose egg — along with just 3 home runs, 13 RBIs, and a .270 OBP that would embarrass a AAA lineup. Sixteen runs scored in a week is Mendoza Line territory for fantasy production. The Wonderdogs' waiver wire activity this week suggests their manager knows things are broken; the question is whether the parts they're grabbing actually fix anything or just rearrange the deck chairs on the Titanic (too soon? never too soon for a sinking-ship metaphor in Week 2).

The Reapers' 75 strikeouts this week actually *tied* the season record for most punchouts by a team in a single week — set, interestingly enough, by the **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** — though the Twins had 77 in that outing, so the record technically holds. Still, 75 Ks in a week is the kind of number that makes other managers quietly check if any of the Reapers' pitchers are available in a trade, then close their laptop in defeat when they realize they're not.

---

## Matchup Deep-Dives

### Sugar Land Skeeters def. Hiroshima Radiation, 8–1

The **Sugar Land Skeeters** scored *40 runs* this week. Forty. That's the most offensive output of the young season and it wasn't particularly close — the **Hiroshima Radiation** put up respectable numbers from Carlos Correa and Schwarber but simply couldn't keep pace with a team that's currently playing like the '27 Yankees decided to also build a competent pitching staff (2.53 ERA, 7 saves/holds). The Radiation drops to a concerning early-season hole and needs to find run production fast before this becomes a real problem.

### Wisconsin Rapids Reapers def. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs, 9–0

Already covered above, but let me just add: the **Wonderdogs** have now been outscored badly across multiple weeks. The waiver wire desperation is real and it's visible. Regroup or spiral — those are the only two paths here.

### Riverside Roughnecks def. Buttville Skidmarks, 7–3

The **Riverside Roughnecks** put up 38 runs, 10 homers, and a .365 OBP — this is a team that looked shaky in Week 1 and has now responded like someone who got bad reviews on Yelp and decided to three-star their own restaurant back into relevance. The **Buttville Skidmarks** got Bryan Reynolds production but the pitching staff couldn't hold the fort. Next week's **Benchwarmers** matchup for the Roughnecks is effectively a bye week given the circumstances.

### North Correa def. Albuquerque Bombers, 7–3

Oneil Cruz and Ohtani doing Oneil Cruz and Ohtani things: 11 home runs, 38 RBIs, a 2.85 ERA. **North Correa** is built like a team that wins categories the way a good poker player wins pots — methodically, without drama, and slightly infuriatingly. The **Albuquerque Bombers** had 7 stolen bases (best in the matchup) and 63 strikeouts from their staff but it wasn't enough. Kyle Harrison was reportedly brilliant, per our planning notes, and *still lost* — that's a tough sentence to read on a Monday morning.

### Abbotsford-Colby Twins def. Canberra Wombats, 6–4

**Canberra Wombats** fans: your team scored 31 runs, hit 9 homers, and got 7 saves/holds from their staff. Jose Altuve posted a .577 OBP. AND YOU STILL LOST. Why? Because the **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** threw 80 innings, went 77 Ks deep, and posted a 2.25 ERA with 7 quality starts. This was a pitching-wins-championships argument delivered in real time. The Wombats need more innings desperately — 59.1 IP isn't enough to compete with a staff like this.

### General Hospital def. Athletic Park Dragons, 6–4

**General Hospital** posted the best ERA by any winning team this season — 2.10 — and combined it with a .363 OBP and 30 runs. Aaron Judge and James Wood showed up for the **Athletic Park Dragons** but the pitching (4.30 ERA, 1.46 WHIP) was the story. The Dragons feel like a team waiting to turn a corner that keeps moving.

---

## Lucky/Unlucky Team of the Week

**Lucky Team: Benchwarmers.** A 4.89 ERA and the second-lowest strikeout total among losers this week, and they still *almost won*. Their luck delta of +0.58 was the highest in the league — meaning the gap between what they deserved and what they nearly got was enormous. Enjoy the moral victory, I guess?

**Unlucky Team: General Hospital.** This is genuinely frustrating to type. A 2.10 ERA — the best ERA by any winner this season — a .363 OBP, and strong run production, and their expected win share (0.92) was way out ahead of their actual result (0.40). The stats said dominant; the schedule said *not so fast*. **General Hospital** is quietly one of the most dangerous teams in this league and they're not getting enough credit for it.

---

## No Records Broken This Week

The **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** came close — their 75 strikeouts this week *tied* the all-time single-week strikeout record for this league, which was set by the **Abbotsford-Colby Twins**. But "tied" is not "broke," and the Twins' 77 Ks still stand as the benchmark. Give the Reapers credit for getting there, but the record belongs to the Twins until further notice.

---

## Manager Spotlight: Abbotsford-Colby Twins

If you're sleeping on the **Abbotsford-Colby Twins**, you are making a catastrophic scheduling error with your attention. Currently sitting at No. 6 in the power rankings — which feels a full two spots too low, honestly — they just went 6–4 against a **Canberra Wombats** team that scored 31 runs and still lost, because the Twins' pitching staff is quietly one of the most dangerous weapons in this league. Eighty innings pitched in a week. *Eighty.* Yordan Alvarez posted a .600 OBP (that's not normal, that's unhinged in the best way), and their 77 strikeouts are the benchmark everyone else is chasing. The Twins are 2–0, their pitching metrics are getting better not worse, and they're the kind of team that doesn't announce itself until it's already in your house. Playoff contender. Bookmark it.

---

## Trade Value Watch

**Yordan Alvarez (Abbotsford-Colby Twins):** Posted a .600 OBP this week while anchoring a winning effort — the man is currently operating in a different zip code than the rest of the league's hitters. **HOLD.** If you have him, you're not moving him. If you don't, the asking price is already too high.

**Corbin Carroll (Benchwarmers):** Carroll is producing in a losing environment, which is both impressive and slightly heartbreaking — like being the best actor in a movie that goes straight to streaming. **BUY** if you can get him at a discount while his team's record keeps the price down.

**Kyle Harrison (Albuquerque Bombers):** Brilliant week, team still lost 7–3. Harrison is the definition of a pitcher whose personal stats mask a team context problem. **HOLD** — the upside is real, but the Bombers need to build around him before he becomes a trade chip someone else cashes in.

---

## Power Rankings

1. **Sugar Land Skeeters** | Dominant 8–1 win, league-best 40 runs, and consistent top-tier category production across two weeks | ⬆️
2. **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** | Historic 9–0 sweep with elite pitching metrics; 75 Ks and 0.84 WHIP are borderline untouchable | ⬆️
3. **Riverside Roughnecks** | Explosive 38-run week and 7–3 win signal a team hitting its stride after a slow Week 1 | ⬆️
4. **North Correa** | Oneil Cruz and Ohtani carried a 7–3 win; pitching depth remains a slight concern but offense is elite | ➡️
5. **General Hospital** | Posted the best ERA of any winner this season (2.10) and wins despite tough luck; real contender | ⬆️
6. **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP and team-best 80 IP anchored a 6–4 win; pitching staff is a weapon | ➡️
7. **BaseballPro77** | Scraped out a 5–4 win with ugly pitching; two straight wins but underlying stats are volatile | ⬇️
8. **Hiroshima Radiation** | Carlos Correa and Schwarber showed up but the offense collapsed late; 8–1 blowout loss hurts | ⬇️
9. **Albuquerque Bombers** | Kyle Harrison was brilliant but the team still fell 7–3; aggressive waiver activity shows urgency | ➡️
10. **Athletic Park Dragons** | Aaron Judge and James Wood couldn't overcome poor pitching in a 6–4 loss; floor feels limited | ⬇️
11. **Buttville Skidmarks** | Bryan Reynolds had a nice week but a 7–3 beatdown exposes real pitching and depth concerns | ➡️
12. **Benchwarmers** | Corbin Carroll is producing but the pitching staff is a liability; barely lost what they should have won easily | ➡️
13. **Canberra Wombats** | Altuve (.577 OBP) and Henderson were stars but 59.1 IP and weak saves cost them the match | ➡️
14. **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** | A 9–0 shutout loss with zero stolen bases and a .270 OBP; desperate waiver moves needed immediately | ⬇️

---

## Waiver Wire

Nothing notable moved this week — or at least nothing worth crowning with a letter grade. The **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** were reportedly active, which tracks given their 9–0 catastrophe, but until we see what they actually added, we're holding judgment. Check back next week when the dust settles and the add/drop carnage becomes clear. (Or when the Wonderdogs steal someone's closer and immediately get hurt. Either one.)
